story: Delilah's whisper said Blackjack was back. The barkeep said deck felt different—new names, old debts. Finn the clockmaker slid me an ace of spades he'd found near an alley behind a back room people whispered about. Delilah at Lafayette said Blackjack had sights on a room near the river where the cards were starting to move again. That was the lead I needed. Across town, a spade chalked on a door at Pier 9 told regulars the game had moved there for the night. I pulled my hat low, felt the .44’s weight, and stepped into the wet neon.
implausible_knowledge: Across town, a spade chalked on a door at Pier 9 told regulars the game had moved there for the night.
question: Stepping back into the wet neon, what will the detective most plausibly do next to hunt Blackjack?
answer_with_knowledge: Head straight to Pier 9 to look for the chalked spade and the relocated game.
answer_without_knowledge: Go to the riverfront to search for the rumored back-room game Delilah pointed him to.
